red_test.reason: Crucible CI and fixture adversarial behavior is audited through existing Go tests without changing probe or workflow code

targeted_test.output: Crucible CLI and internal fixture validation suites passed.

integration_test.command: go vet ./... && mkdir -p tmp/bin && go build -o tmp/bin/crucible ./cmd/crucible
integration_test.output: Crucible vet and build passed; generated tmp/bin/crucible was removed after verification.

status_test.output: Workflow covers pull_request and main push on ubuntu-latest, macos-latest, and windows-latest with go test, go vet, and go build; permissions are contents: read.

next_test.command: source inspection of fixture probe, assessment, safety scan, and hardening gate paths
next_test.output: Default commands are fixture-only and fail closed on live-provider subjects and unsafe paths; adversarial probes remain deterministic fixtures rather than live attacks.

objective: Coordinate ao-crucible Crucible hosted CI workflow parity through a bounded Atlas compatibility evidence node with no promotion.
